transcript.text: Good, how are you?
Doing pretty well, thank you.
I'm Andy, nice to meet you.
I am the head of, can you hear me?
Okay.
Sorry, I thought you were looking at something intently.
I didn't know if I was on the radar.
So I'm the head of customer operations for GrowthX, and it sounds like I was just looking at your LinkedIn.
I think we have both been at our new roles for the same amount of time.
Am I correct in seeing that you joined Flosum about a month ago?
About a month and a half, two months.
Okay, I saw your LinkedIn posts.
Yeah, so I'm relatively new to GrowthX leading customer operations, and he's hopping from another call.
No worries.
But we basically scheduled these calls as...
...because...
Really high-level feedback sessions to understand how the partnership's going, how we're performing against deliverables.
I would love to get a little bit of context on your goals as you're getting ramped in this new role and how you're thinking about utilizing GrowthX, especially stepping into this partnership.
So that's kind of the goal today.
And really, like, all feedback is good feedback.
Don't sugarcoat anything if you're feeling like we're not delivering on something or you're frustrated.
But also want to talk a little bit about, like, where you're going in terms of your strategy.
And if we have some time, if you haven't seen it yet, we have a new internal platform that will soon be released to our clients as well.
So we can show you that, too, the end.
Cool.
Cool.
Where do you want to start?
you want me to just put it in?
I don't
Just start just like how you're thinking about like how you came into the GrowthX partnership and how you're thinking about it in the context of your role.
So I came to the GrowthX partnership as this was my very first meeting on my very first day.
I was actually offered the job on Friday and I started on Monday so I could join this call.
So to be very honest, I've been at, you know, I've been on the ground for it from the beginning.
But I'm also just, you know, that feeling of being in a month and a half that you feel overly blind on everything.
So what I would say is that I came into it with an understanding of what we were going to do.
Not always a huge fan of a bunch of content being posted at once, but also knowing that we need to do that to get the hits that we want to get to.
And I came into it with...
So let's say, Thank
The company already sort of having a direction on which way that they wanted to go with the relationship.
And I don't mean that in a bad way.
just mean sort of picking up from that process, you know, primarily focusing on one side of our product line as opposed to brand or anything like that.
So I would say right now, like when I got the invite to have the meeting, I was like, okay, I don't know if I'm going to be like the best at this, but but happy to have the convo.
And, you know, I look at it as another form of content for us to get it on the board, really to get our name out there.
We need to be showing up in different places, especially via SEO, using LLMs and all that stuff.
So I am totally on board with it and just trying to get my feet underneath me and the rest of it.
Yeah, well, let's talk about kind of the rest of it then.
It...
I'm curious where you're spending a lot of your time and how you're thinking about the strategy writ large for the org and what you're leading.
Yeah, I'm actually working on it right now.
mean, not right now, but looking at strategy, generally speaking, over H2, there's always a lot of fluctuation.
And with a company of our size, there's always going to be a lot of change.
So ultimately, my goals right now are for us to drive revenue, to build brand awareness.
And I have a couple other ones, but really increasing pipeline stage conversions are big ones, too.
Those are three things, as I look at it, that is where we're going to really make hay, is brand awareness and pipeline stage.
Conversions.
Everything else rolls up to pipeline, but, you know, I think that's really what I'm looking at.
We're able to do content.
The team is able to put together different things, but having extra content is never a problem.
Yeah, I mean, I guess there are different ways to think about, like, the pipeline stage conversions, and you guys are putting out content and GrowthX is supporting.

So how, I mean, do you feel like you are just having GrowthX kind of execute on what we were hired for, or are you interested and curious about, like, different strategies that we can execute on as you're evolving your thinking around your approach?
So I don't know, is the main question there.
What we'll say, and this is not, I don't think you were asking this, but Yeah.
I am

We're in the position to add more money to our contract, but as we're going through it, think once we get sort of to a good volume of content, know, being able to mix in other topics would be super helpful.
Yeah.

And I don't like I think where GrowthX is today, quite honestly, is not like we're thinking a lot about experimentation and moving around kind of the money that you're spending as we ramp up these partnerships because there is so much changing all the time.
So I was curious, like, if you had thought about different directions that we could go in on this stuff.
I also like I'll say one thing that we're hearing because we're doing a lot of these feedback conversations right now is as we do ramp up content and thinking about pipeline conversions.

Building that long tail on specific niches where you're already seeing a lot of impressions or you're seeing some success and building up the gated content beyond that and kind of the different ways that we can get people through that pipeline and to get on a sales call could be another way that we evolve the partnership.
That's not necessarily like more money or different, you know, it's just we want to be flexible.
And so I'm curious, having come in with like somebody set the initial strategy, how like I want to make clear that like we're open to change, too.
And so I'm curious how you you're thinking about your bigger picture.
Yeah.
No, I think I think having more in there would be helpful for sure.
I just don't know.
I mean, I was actually just in our.
Channel, because I know there was some, I know we've got about 14 published right now.
There's a whole bunch that we've got in, you know, building out and we've got a whole bunch of other stuff and like, ready to start and ready for review and like all that stuff.
So I think for me, it's more just like, hey, just, you know, what, how many do we get to when it's, because, because I'll look at you guys as the experts.
When do we get to a point where it makes sense for us to start adding some other stuff and some other stories, some other pieces.
And I know that you all have, you know, lots of detail and things on the back end.
So it's just for me, I think that's just kind of my biggest thing is I don't really know what I don't know right now.
unfortunately my answer.
Well, and that's, yeah, that's certainly like a larger conversation that we should be having.
We can start to have more often with you as we hit our pace.
In terms of that, those.
Initial articles that have been published and that sort of calibration, I guess we're just out of calibration phase now.
How are you feeling about our ability to hit your voice and your sort of goal of positioning so far?
I think so far we're looking good.
It takes the time for it to be honed and cleaned up and all of that.
I will say that I read through several at the beginning that I had to stop reading just because I was still getting my head wrapped around all the stuff.
And it's hard to look at product stuff as well as tone when you're still getting up to speed.
But as I learned through it, I did see stuff was starting to get fixed up and cleaned up.
And it does look like we're getting to a good point there.
I don't have any issues with it right now.
The only things that I've really had...
Any concerns with were more so about the product stuff, which I, that's not been you guys yet.
That's more about on just everything learning and wanting to have our, our product team and our product owners look at it.
So I think that's been kind of one of the big bottlenecks is just when we have a, you know, a technical conversation about something, I want to make sure that they're a part of it.
Yeah.
I'm just taking notes.
Yeah, no problem.
All right.
So I know, um, you're just getting your feet under you.
So actually what I'd like to do maybe is I'm going to jump into this a little bit early.
Okay.
But we also have, um, Um, I don't know.
I don't know if the team told you, but last week, we stepped into our own workflow platform, where we're executing the workflows on our own code base for all of our accounts now.
Yeah, they had mentioned something similar.
They had mentioned that.
I didn't know if they had said specifically it was happening last week, but they had said that they were working through some of that.

Yeah, so I'd love to show you what that looks like on your end, and then maybe that will give you some ideas as you're firming up your footing on what comes next for how you best leverage us in terms of the workflows that we're able to run and what we're running now for you.
And again, keep in mind, this is all just to say that we can tweak your strategy if you want to.
Like, we're not just like...
But I didn't forget it with you guys.
So I will go ahead and show my screen.
Okay.
You see this?
Yes.
Okay.

So basically what we're doing here is building up, and this is the reason I asked about like how you feel about our brand voice and the way that we've captured so far the calibration for how you're thinking about your brand because we're able to build up artifacts on the organization that all feed into workflow generation against kind of like the keywords and the product positioning that we're delivering content on.
So this is all like super editable and should be something that we're constantly tweaking with you.
CTAs, your target personas, writing guidelines, you know, this is who you are, voice, tone, and style, and, you know, never use those qualifiers or buzzwords.

And you can just see, like, the level of detail that we're going into into all of these so that when we're executing workflows in the pipeline, which is over here, you know, we have, like, an assignment workflow where we haven't run into any of these yet, but we basically, like, can generate assignment briefs here, pulling from, like, using all of the artifacts as, like, underlying content or text.
And then our article generation workflow then pulls in the assignment brief, and this is just the first one that we've run, and we can go, like, one of the beauties.
So this workflow is that we can run as much of it as we want and kind of see the output and then keep going.
So it really centers on the human in the loop aspect, you know, go on internal linking, and we can kind of see how far we go.
And looking at this workflow, you can kind of see, okay, so this is the assignment brief that we've run.
And then we're going back, we're doing research, we're generating the outline, and all of this, again, is editable, you can kind of, oh, hold on.
Okay, they changed the password to inspect the elements.
Of course, as you're doing it live.
Yeah, so I was going to just show you, like, you could kind of, like, see the code base that's underlying.
But it also makes it so that anybody that's managing the account, doesn't have to be an engineer, can kind of edit the inputs.
We're running an article draft, and then we're doing fact checking.
And you can edit all of this without knowing the code base, but then you can inspect the code base.
We can then adapt to your style, generate the internal links, and get, like, an ultimate output.
So each of these pieces is, like, highly editable and customizable.
So if you want to start to tweak as we're generating things, like, any part of you decide that the brand voice needs a little bit of a change.
As you're getting more comfortable, you want us to think differently about the product or positioning.
Like, all of that can then be tweaked within the workflows that can then start to generate that high level of content again.
Changing, like, the base direction and then getting to that high level.
level of output really quickly.
So just wanted to show you this has like changed so much even in the last week that we've been demoing it.
Yeah.
But this will be what we're working on going forward.
You know, we've also got like an article refresher, so we can start to do like SEO analysis on articles.
And again, FAQ generation, all that stuff.
So just to give you an idea of kind of what's possible, the direction that we're going in.
I won't like pressure you to answer more questions because it sounds like you're still firming up like your idea of where we're going.

But thought I'd just give you a sneak peek on that and like, feel free to ask us bounce ideas off of us because your team like should be, we should be thought partners in thinking about this positioning and where you want to go going.
Forward, and we are flexible.
We want to experiment with you.
Okay.
The platform that you just showed, that's just on your guys' end, right?
So that's not something that, I mean, are we going to have access?
It's to be exposed.
Like, I think we're going to give our clients access probably in the next two months.
Oh, okay.
We're ironing things out, making sure that we have, like, a really clear, like, instructions, and the UX is really clean.
And then we'll be exposing it to you guys sooner rather than later.
But I think, like, you should feel free.
Like, I'm going to tell your team that, like, talk through these pieces and kind of do a little bit of a reset on this.
And then there are...
and I don't see, and I probably don't even want necessarily my team in there much.
I would rather we work with you guys so we don't mess anything up.
But that being said, you know, as we work through voice things, which we will...
I think that's a really good conversation to have, and it's really cool to see how in-depth you will have it on there as well.
There was another question that I had.
I forgot what it was.
Oh, the topics in SEO and things like that.
I know that a lot of that came from your team.
You know, I know that the topics that are coming up and being added to it continue to come from your team.
But I guess my question just kind of comes down to how often are we updating some of those keywords and things like that from your end?
To an extent of like, you know, things shift all the time, especially in tech and DevOps and all that.
Like, how often do we go through and tweak and change things?
Yeah, I mean, mean, it...
Is probably like a conversation that you guys should continually be having and we don't have like, depends on the industry, right?
Like those aren't being tweaked very often.

One thing that we're starting to do and I'm in SF this week specifically like for an onsite to talk a little bit about how like the evolution of geo, so how LLM searches are influencing kind of like those results and how we can think differently about that keyword piece, especially.

So we should be, if we haven't yet done a session with you, talking a little bit about what the, like what the questions that people are asking LLMs in your like vertical are and how you guys are ranking in those areas and that should be informing keywords.
That should be something that you guys will probably be doing in the next two to three.
Weeks anyways, because like we're doing a little bit of a deeper training session on it right now.
Okay.
Yeah, we've got like those tools on our end to start to give you answers around those search areas.
That sounds great.
Yeah, I know that that makes sense.
Because I'm looking at them on my end too, but not as much as I was, you the first couple of weeks.
But it's, I think, having those, that detail from you guys would be super helpful as we go.
But yeah, in the next couple of weeks is completely fine.
This is not a, we're on fire and need to get together and talk about stuff.
So.
Yeah.
Yeah, we're working.
I don't know if you've seen, we just put out like a, I think a podcast episode about, we're working with Scrunch.
We have a conversation with the CEO of Scrunch that went up, I think today.
So if you haven't seen it yet, I'll have, I'll have the team send it to you.
But definitely like something that's really top of mind for us too.
So we'll make sure that we're including that.
Great.
Cool.
I mean, I think I appreciate you taking the time.
I think, yeah, it's unfortunate.
I don't, as I said, I don't have a ton of, like, feedback yet.
Just because, you know, I know, like, right now, I think the biggest feedback is probably from your guys' end on, can you please respond to things and get stuff cleaned up?
But I think we've got, you know, a good volume right now in, that got done with review and all the other stuff, so.
Yeah.
Cool.
Well, this is just to let you know that the line of communication is open.
Nothing is, like, off limits.
This, like, our scope of works are meant to be flexible, especially given how young we are.
And so, like, this is a good time for us to be having this conversation.
And, like, don't be afraid to push on.
And things that you want, if you want to change directions a little bit or anything like that.
Sure.
I appreciate that.
I think some of it will be me asking the team, our next call is this week sometime, but on, you know, how many of these should we have done before we start looking at other areas?
Yeah.
Some of that's internal, too, because I know it was our CEO had led with, let's do, let's make sure we're looking at backup and archive and that story, getting that out there.
But I think there's a lot of value in looking at overall keywords and seeing, too, just brand-wise.
So, yeah, and that's definitely something that we do a lot more of, too, is, like, that broader approach and, like, a full keyword analysis.
It sounds like the CEO came in with some specific areas.
So, yeah, let's keep going.
Yeah, because we have our DevOps product, and then we have a backup and archive product that we're trying to make sure we get out into the world.
And I think this was a good way for us to get more content out there and really start to be seeding our name into those conversations.
But I wonder if, you know, we want to look at that, the DevOps product as well.
But that's something that...
And talk about as a team, then I'll talk about internally as well.
Perfect.
Okay.
Well, Matt, thank you for your time.
was great to be with you.
Yeah, you too.
Have safe travels in San Francisco.
Thank you.
And good luck on week six because, yeah, I'm on week five.
Yeah, you know how it is.
Okay.
Well, thank you very much.
We'll talk soon.
Yeah.
Have a good one.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Conduct a high-level feedback session on the GrowthX-Flosum partnership and align on future strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Matt is new to Flosum (1.5-2 months) and still getting up to speed on the GrowthX partnership](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=130.0)
  - [Current focus: drive revenue, build brand awareness, increase pipeline stage conversions](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=270.0)
  - [GrowthX's new internal workflow platform offers potential for more customized content strategy](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=685.0)
  - [Opportunity to expand content focus beyond backup/archive to include DevOps product](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1373.0)

## Topics

### Partnership Context and Goals

  - [Matt joined Flosum \~1.5-2 months ago, started on day of first GrowthX meeting](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=130.0)
  - [Initial strategy set before Matt's arrival, focusing on one product line](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
  - [Current goals: drive revenue, build brand awareness, increase pipeline stage conversions](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=270.0)
  - [14 articles published, more in pipeline at various stages](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=481.0)

### Content Strategy and Execution

  - [Matt open to mixing in other topics once good volume of content is achieved](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=468.0)
  - [Potential to build long-tail content on successful niches, develop gated content](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=407.0)
  - [Initial calibration phase complete; content quality improving](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=562.0)
  - [Some early concerns about product accuracy, requiring internal review](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=597.0)

### GrowthX's New Workflow Platform

  - [Recently implemented internal workflow platform for executing account workflows](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=651.0)
  - [Features include:](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=735.0)
      - [Editable organization artifacts (CTAs, personas, writing guidelines)](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)
      - [Customizable workflow generation (assignments, article creation, fact-checking)](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=796.0)
      - [SEO analysis and FAQ generation capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=976.0)
  - [Platform to be exposed to clients in \~2 months](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1034.0)

### Keyword and SEO Strategy

  - [Matt inquired about frequency of keyword updates](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1112.0)
  - [GrowthX planning session on LLM search influence on keywords](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1152.0)
  - [Upcoming deeper training on LLM-related search areas in next 2-3 weeks](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1194.0)

### Product Focus and Content Direction

  - [Current emphasis on backup and archive product content](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1360.0)
  - [Potential to expand focus to include DevOps product](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1373.0)
  - [Matt to discuss internally about balancing product focus and overall brand keywords](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1379.0)

## Next Steps

  - [GrowthX team to initiate conversation about expanding content topics beyond initial focus](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=379.0)
  - [Schedule session on LLM search influence and keyword strategy in coming weeks](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1194.0)
  - [GrowthX team to send recent podcast episode with Scrunch CEO to Matt](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1235.0)
  - [Matt to discuss internally about expanding content focus to DevOps product](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1373.0)
  - [Continue refining content quality and brand voice](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=562.0)
  - [Plan for eventual client access to GrowthX's workflow platform](https://fathom.video/share/p2ZXbxQVrYsznhoakqqkDy-CtszrPpBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1034.0)

action_items.description: Schedule LLM search/keyword ranking session w/ Flosum team (next 2-3 weeks)
Have team send Scrunch CEO podcast episode link to Matt
Discuss w/ team - how many backup/archive articles before expanding to other areas (e.g. DevOps product)
